name: Gavin Moriarty
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary analog environments • Environmental change • Microbial life detection
bio: Gavin Moriarty is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, where his research focuses on remote sensing and planetary analog environments, particularly in Antarctica’s McMurdo Dry Valleys. He works under Dr. Mark Salvatore, contributing to projects that use satellite and drone data to study environmental change and identify microbial life in extreme environments as analogs for extraterrestrial conditions. Moriarty has experience in astronomy research, public outreach, and telescope operations, including work at Lowell Observatory and leadership roles such as President of NAU’s Astronomy Club. In addition to his academic work, he is the founder and director of Leaf Philanthropy, a nonprofit organization dedicated to fundraising for charitable causes.
